work_order.objective:
Project: aetherion-maze (maze-groundup HTML5 canvas), 64x64 tiles. Input: Reference board image only (not build-ready). Do NOT attempt sprite integration from it. We must keep implementation moving without broken mapping.

Your tasks:
1) Asset-readiness verdict for the game code: identify loader/build assumptions that expected a real spritesheet/atlas; state how we will operate until proper assets arrive.
2) Proposed interim approach:
   - Keep terrain and gameplay functioning with placeholders (cozy terrain or procedural tiles) and/or temporary flat-color icons for entities.
   - Ensure the code path uses manifest-first loading when an atlas is provided, and otherwise falls back to safe placeholders without errors.
   - Confirm only floor/walls are in TileMap draws; all else are entities/layers with spacing rules.
3) Minimal code change plan (short diffs or bullet points) to:
   - Harden Skins/Sprites to prefer JSON atlas (if present), ignore autoslice by default, and fall back to placeholders if any role is missing.
   - Provide a stub catalog (assets/painted/catalog.json) format mapping logical roles to atlas frame IDs. If not present, code should continue gracefully.
   - Add a guard so traps/items/enemies never render blank (draw a small color-coded square with a letter if sprite missing: e.g., T for trap, E for enemy, I for item).
4) Deliverables:
   - A concise GAME_NOTES.md suitable to drop into maze-groundup/docs/ with the interim plan, assumptions, and guard behavior.
   - A snippet for a role->placeholder color map we can paste into renderer/skins.

Keep it tight, production-minded, and aligned with HTML5 canvas (no Godot).

delivery_model.strategy_label: Lead -> Creative -> Game Dev -> App Dev

stage_guidance.operator: Own scope, freeze the delivery target, and keep the handoffs honest.
stage_guidance.creative-dev: Define the creative target, vision guardrails, and artifact acceptance language before implementation drifts.
stage_guidance.game-dev: Own the playable build path and converge the actual game implementation on the chosen target artifact.
stage_guidance.app-dev: Own the app, tooling, portal, and review-loop support needed to make the chosen artifact reviewable and repeatable.

handoff_contract: freeze one primary delivery target before downstream implementation expands scope
treat creative-dev as the vision and acceptance guardrail for downstream lanes
treat game-dev as the owner of the playable implementation path once the vision is frozen
treat app-dev as the owner of portal, tooling, packaging, and review-loop support after the build path is clear
require each lane to leave a concrete handoff for the next lane instead of sending the operator back into open-ended review

verification_required: run the smallest local verification possible before claiming success
classify each referenced art input as reference board, loose sprites, uniform grid sheet, atlas plus manifest, or unknown before integrating it
if an image includes labels, section dividers, notes, or a presentation background, state that it is a reference board and not a build-ready runtime sheet
do not claim sprite integration is complete until a machine-usable artifact exists such as loose transparent sprites, a uniform grid sheet with proven cell geometry, or an atlas with manifest
work_order.project_now: Lead orchestration complete for: Converge Aetherion Maze under one lead-owned path that runs creative-dev first, then game-dev, then app-dev, with one primary delivery target and no parallel rebuild drift.
work_order.project_next: Operator selects the next execution slice from the orchestration artifact and keeps one primary delivery target.

implemented: Loaded relevant skills for HTML5 maze QA and cozy/DPR contracts to align with our renderer and placeholder approach.
Audited maze-groundup code to identify asset loader assumptions and poster-board risks:
sprites.js defaults to autoslice unless explicitly disabled by caller.
skins.js already prefers manifest rects (if present), disables autoslice by default for painted, and can map poster sheets via heuristics when poster mode is on.

renderer.js already draws safe, color-coded placeholders with role letters for all entities when sprites are missing (never-blank guarantee). Floor/walls draw in the grid pass only; entities draw in later passes.
assets/sprite_catalog.json and assets/atlas_manifest.json both reference poster_sheet.png (a reference board), which we must treat as non-runtime by default.
Updated docs/ GAME_NOTES.md in maze-groundup with the interim plan, explicit poster gating requirement, catalog priority, and guard behavior.
verified: Only floor/walls are drawn during the grid pass; all items, powerups, traps, enemies, and player render as entities after the grid (renderer.js lines ~54–116 for terrain; ~118–168 for entities).

Placeholder guards: renderer draws letter-badged colored placeholders for traps (T), enemies (E), items (I), portal (P), and hero (H) when sprites are missing (renderer.js lines ~118–168). This satisfies the “never render blank” requirement.
Skins/Sprites behavior:
skins.js prefers manifest rects (buildRectsFromManifest) when present and autoslice is disabled by default for painted unless ?autoguess=1.
sprites.js accepts allowAutoslice and rects to enforce manifest-first loading and no-heuristic fallback.
Deliverable artifact: maze-groundup/docs/GAME_NOTES.md is present and updated with the interim plan and guard behavior.

Asset classification: poster_sheet.png is a reference board (not a build-ready atlas). Both assets/sprite_catalog.json and assets/atlas_manifest.json currently target poster_sheet.png — not acceptable as a default runtime source.
Asset-readiness verdict (for the game code)

Current build includes a poster reference board (poster_sheet.png) plus two JSONs pointing to it: assets/sprite_catalog.json and assets/atlas_manifest.json. These are not production-safe runtime inputs unless deliberately toggled for testing. Relying on them would violate “no sprite integration from reference boards.”
Loader assumptions expecting a real spritesheet/atlas:
sprites.js can “autoslice” — heuristic and unsafe for poster boards with labels/dividers.
skins.js can detect “poster” and attempt a poster mapping heuristic; this must not be default.
How we will operate until proper assets arrive:
Default to cozy/procedural terrain for floor/walls and lettered placeholders for all entities.
Manifest-first JSON atlas usage stays available, but only when the texture is a real atlas (not poster) or when explicitly flagged for testing (?poster=1).
Continue using 64x64 tiles; no entities drawn in the TileMap pass.
Proposed interim approach
Terrain and gameplay remain fully functional with cozy/procedural floor/walls and placeholder entities.
Loader policy:
Prefer JSON atlas + explicit rects (manifest-first) when provided and not pointing at poster.
Prefer assets/painted/catalog.json as the first mapping source; fall back to assets/sprite_catalog.json.
Ignore autoslice by default; only allow ?autoguess=1 for debug.
Treat any “poster” texture as reference-only unless explicitly opted in via ?poster=1.
Minimal code change plan (short diffs/bullets)
skins.js
Gate poster mode behind a query flag and don’t enable from filenames:
Before:
MF_POSTER_MODE = true if chosenTexture includes 'poster' OR query requested poster.
After:
Only enable poster when ?poster=1 (or ?usePoster=1). If manifest/catalog references a 'poster' texture and no poster flag is set, treat as absent and fall back to cozy/placeholder.
Catalog priority:
Try './assets/painted/catalog.json' first; if missing, fall back to './assets/sprite_catalog.json'.
Manifest-first with non-poster texture:
If assets/atlas_manifest.json exists and its texture_file is NOT a poster file, call loadSprites(url, { rects: buildRectsFromManifest(man), allowAutoslice:false }).
Missing-role gaps:
Do not auto-guess roles unless ?autoguess=1. Let renderer placeholders draw (guards remain intact).
Example diff hunk:
Compute posterAllowed and chosenTexture:
const posterAllowed = !!(q && (q.get('poster')==='1' || q.get('usePoster')==='1'));
const rawTexture = (manifestTexture) ? manifestTexture : (atlasTexture || manifestTexture || 'spritesheet.png');
const chosenTexture = (!posterAllowed && /poster/i.test(rawTexture)) ? 'spritesheet.png' : rawTexture;
window.MF_POSTER_MODE = !!(posterAllowed && /poster/i.test(chosenTexture));
Catalog load order:
First try fetch('./assets/painted/catalog.json?v=' + Date.now()); if absent, use './assets/sprite_catalog.json'.
sprites.js
Keep current interface; enforce allowAutoslice:false from skins.js for painted path by default.
No change needed if callers always pass allowAutoslice:false except for ?autoguess=1.
flags.js (optional)
Consider defaulting MF_SKIN to 'cozy' for extra safety until a real atlas lands. Not strictly required if poster gating above is applied correctly; either path is acceptable.
Stub catalog format (assets/painted/catalog.json)
Present and correct as a production-facing contract for creative-dev. Maps logical roles to integer frame indices on a real atlas PNG, with tile_size=64.
Role -> placeholder color map (snippet to paste into renderer/skins)
export const PLACEHOLDER_COLORS = {
enemy: '#ff4a4a', // E
trap: '#c7a56b', // T
portal: '#c19cff', // P
crystal: '#9be0ff', // I
key: '#f5d46b', // I
potion: '#8ef0a8', // I
shield: '#9bbcf7', // I
hero: '#10a1ff' // H
